properties.name: Saudi Arabia
properties.title: Saudi Arabia
properties.description: Regional Sunni power and Iran's primary rival. Riyadh views Iranian expansionism as an existential threat. The two countries cut diplomatic ties in 2016, restoring them only in 2023 via Chinese brokerage. Saudi Arabia hosts major US air assets and has tacitly supported strikes on Iranian military infrastructure.

properties.name: UAE
properties.title: United Arab Emirates
properties.description: A pragmatic actor maintaining economic ties with Iran while hosting US forces. Dubai handles significant Iran-linked trade despite sanctions. The UAE normalised relations with Israel in 2020 and pursues quiet diplomacy while hosting CENTCOM's forward headquarters at Al Dhafra Air Base.

properties.name: Qatar
properties.title: Qatar
properties.description: Host of Al Udeid Air Base — the largest US military installation in the Middle East — yet maintains open dialogue with Iran. The two countries share the world's largest natural gas field. Qatar has served as a backchannel mediator in multiple regional crises.

properties.name: Kuwait
properties.title: Kuwait
properties.description: Hosts US Army and Air Force assets at Camp Arifjan and Ali Al Salem. Maintains a low-profile foreign policy shaped by proximity to both Iran and Iraq. Its large Shia population — roughly 30% — complicates a fully anti-Iran stance.

properties.name: Bahrain
properties.title: Bahrain
properties.description: Home to the US Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters. Bahrain's Sunni royal family governs a majority-Shia population and views Iran as a destabilising force. In 2011, Saudi-led GCC forces entered Bahrain to suppress protests the government linked to Iranian influence.

properties.name: Oman
properties.title: Oman
properties.description: The Gulf's indispensable mediator. Oman has maintained unbroken diplomatic relations with Iran throughout every crisis and has hosted secret US-Iran talks including contacts that led to the 2015 nuclear deal. Oman pursues strict non-alignment — it is not a member of any anti-Iran coalition.

properties.name: Iraq
properties.title: Iraq
properties.description: Iran's most significant regional ally and a primary conduit for Iranian influence into the Arab world. Iraq's Shia-dominated government maintains close ties with Tehran while also hosting a US military presence. Iranian-backed militias (PMF) operate alongside — and sometimes in tension with — the Iraqi state.

properties.name: Iran
properties.title: Iran
properties.description: The central actor in regional geopolitics. Iran projects influence through a network of allied militias — Hezbollah in Lebanon, Hamas in Gaza, the Houthis in Yemen, and PMF in Iraq — collectively known as the Axis of Resistance. Iran's nuclear programme and geographic control of the Strait of Hormuz make it the region's most consequential state actor.

properties.name: Yemen
properties.title: Yemen — Houthi Movement
properties.description: The Houthi movement receives arms, funding, and training from Iran. Since 2023 the Houthis have launched repeated attacks on Red Sea shipping, disrupting global trade routes and drawing US and UK military responses. Yemen represents Iran's most active proxy front.

properties.name: Syria
properties.title: Syria
properties.description: A critical node in Iran's land corridor to Lebanon. Iran intervened decisively to preserve the Assad government during the civil war. Syria provides transit routes for weapons to Hezbollah and hosts Iranian military advisors and IRGC positions. Israeli strikes on Iranian-linked targets in Syria have become routine.

properties.name: Lebanon
properties.title: Lebanon — Hezbollah
properties.description: Hezbollah, Iran's most capable proxy, operates as a state within a state. Formed with IRGC assistance in 1982, Hezbollah's missile arsenal is estimated at over 150,000 rockets. It fought a devastating war with Israel in 2024. Lebanon's formal government has limited control over Hezbollah's military decisions.

properties.name: Gaza
properties.title: Gaza — Hamas
properties.description: Hamas receives funding and weapons from Iran. The October 2023 attacks and subsequent Gaza war drew Iran into public confrontation with Israel for the first time, including direct missile exchanges in April and October 2024.

properties.name: Israel
properties.title: Israel
properties.description: Iran's most direct adversary and the state that carried out the 2025 strikes. Israel views a nuclear-armed Iran as an existential threat and has conducted covert operations — including assassinations of nuclear scientists — for over a decade. The 2024 direct missile exchanges marked a fundamental shift from shadow war to open confrontation.

properties.name: Al Udeid
properties.title: Al Udeid Air Base — Qatar
properties.description: The largest US military base in the Middle East by personnel. Home to CENTCOM's forward headquarters and the Combined Air Operations Center coordinating all US and coalition air operations across the region. Hosts B-52 bombers, KC-135 tankers, and fighter squadrons.

properties.name: NSA Bahrain
properties.title: Naval Support Activity Bahrain
properties.description: Headquarters of US Naval Forces Central Command and the Fifth Fleet. Primary US naval hub for the Persian Gulf, Red Sea, and Arabian Sea. Hosts carrier strike group support and patrol vessel operations.

properties.name: Al Dhafra
properties.title: Al Dhafra Air Base — UAE
properties.description: Major US Air Force installation hosting U-2 reconnaissance aircraft, F-35 fighters, and tanker assets. Provides surveillance coverage across the Gulf and into Iranian airspace.

properties.name: Camp Arifjan
properties.title: Camp Arifjan — Kuwait
properties.description: Primary US Army logistics and forward positioning base in the Gulf. Hosts the Army's Theater Sustainment Command and prepositioned equipment for rapid regional deployment.
